prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='svr', seasonality_champion='CWT_rf', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.5051) and high seasonality capture variance (0.7512), with the true series showing varying trend directions across folds. Early horizon specialists (NaiveSeasonal, ARIMA) differ from late horizon specialists (svr, FT_catboost), indicating horizon-specific model preferences. Given both high disagreement and high seasonal variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage complementary strengths while mitigating individual model weaknesses.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "svr",
  "seasonality_champion": "CWT_rf",
  "overall_champion": "FT_catboost",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "NaiveSeasonal", "late": "svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["FT_catboost", "svr", "ONLY_FT_rf", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_svr", "FT_svr", "catboost", "ARIMA"],
  "tier2_models": ["NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "CWT_catboost", "DWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "DWT_catboost", "NaiveSeasonal", "rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.5051,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.5051) and high seasonality capture variance (0.7512), with the true series showing varying trend directions across folds. Early horizon specialists (NaiveSeasonal, ARIMA) differ from late horizon specialists (svr, FT_catboost), indicating horizon-specific model preferences. Given both high disagreement and high seasonal variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage complementary strengths while mitigating individual model weaknesses."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). All candidates are from valid_candidate_names. Score margin between top2 is small (0.004), indicating competitive set. No leakage detected - selection candidates use validation data, baseline never leaks, weighted uses anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection. Universe leaderboards show topk_mean_per_horizon_k2 and inverse_rmse_weights_k2 variants perform well but current set already has strong performers. No pruning needed (only 4 candidates, no single type >=4).",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["topk_mean_per_horizon_k2", "inverse_rmse_weights_k2_sh0.35"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Universe leaderboards show topk_mean_per_horizon_k2 (RMSE=2540.61) and inverse_rmse_weights_k2_sh0.35 outperform current candidates. n_windows=3 is low, so following recommended_knobs: stronger shrinkage and smaller k. Current set has good diversity but could benefit from k2 variants which show better performance in universe. relative_spread_mean=0.15 (<0.25) means disagreement is moderate, not extreme.", "changes": ["added topk_mean_per_horizon_k2 for better RMSE from universe leaderboard", "added inverse_rmse_weights_k2_sh0.35 with higher shrinkage for low n_windows"], "when_good": ""}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.5051) and high seasonality capture variance (0.7512), with the true series showing varying trend directions across folds. Early horizon specialists (NaiveSeasonal, ARIMA) differ from late horizon specialists (svr, FT_catboost), indicating horizon-specific model preferences. Given both high disagreement and high seasonal variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage complementary strengths while mitigating individual model weaknesses.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates high model diversity across horizons. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination due to rmse_spread_ratio=0.5051 and seasonality_corr_variance=0.7512. horizon_heterogeneous=true suggests top-k selection with shrinkage≥0.3. DBA handles phase-shifted peaks from trend_champions (svr, FT_catboost) vs seasonality_champions (CWT_rf, NaiveSeasonal). best_per_horizon_by_validation captures early/late horizon specialists. Top-k=3 balances stability with 3 windows available.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). All candidates are from valid_candidate_names. Score margin between top2 is small (0.004), indicating competitive set. No leakage detected - selection candidates use validation data, baseline never leaks, weighted uses anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection. Universe leaderboards show topk_mean_per_horizon_k2 and inverse_rmse_weights_k2 variants perform well but current set already has strong performers. No pruning needed (only 4 candidates, no single type >=4).

statistician.rationale: Universe leaderboards show topk_mean_per_horizon_k2 (RMSE=2540.61) and inverse_rmse_weights_k2_sh0.35 outperform current candidates. n_windows=3 is low, so following recommended_knobs: stronger shrinkage and smaller k. Current set has good diversity but could benefit from k2 variants which show better performance in universe. relative_spread_mean=0.15 (<0.25) means disagreement is moderate, not extreme.
changes: added topk_mean_per_horizon_k2 for better RMSE from universe leaderboard
added inverse_rmse_weights_k2_sh0.35 with higher shrinkage for low n_windows
